title: quercus sessilis f. sublobata
intro: Quercus sessilis f. sublobata, also known as a form of the sessile oak, is a deciduous tree native to Japan, characterized by its unique branching structure. The leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus sessilis f. sublobata include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations involving this plant typically include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate specific health complaints.
